intro: Backblaze B2 enters at $0 against Mux at $20/mo. Both tools position themselves for image/video heavy applications, so the decision rarely comes down to surface features. In practice, Backblaze B2 — S3-compatible object storage at $0.006/GB with free egress to Cloudflare CDN partners. Mux — Video infrastructure API for adaptive bitrate streaming, real-time data, and ML-powered quality scoring. Mux scores 5/5 on developer experience versus 4/5 for Backblaze B2. Backblaze B2 rates 5/5 on transparency versus 4/5 for Mux. Both tools list image/video heavy applications as a headline strength, which is why pricing and lock-in usually decide the call. Both tools surface overlapping when-to-use scenarios, including image/video heavy applications. The honest trade-off: Backblaze B2 is not built for small projects, and Mux is not the right call for small projects, so neither is a universal default. Transparency scores land at 5/5 for Backblaze B2 and 4/5 for Mux, which reflects how openly each tool documents pricing, limits, and terms.
quick_take: Backblaze B2 is for image/video heavy applications; Mux overlaps on the same job; decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Backblaze B2 if image/video heavy applications and the $0 entry price fits your budget.
verdict_b: Choose Mux if image/video heavy applications and the $20/mo entry price fits your budget.

faq.q: Is Backblaze B2 cheaper than Mux?
faq.a: Based on the listed entry prices, yes: Backblaze B2 starts at $0 while Mux starts at $20/mo. Actual monthly cost depends on volume and plan tier, so model your real usage before switching. Enterprise quotes can reshuffle the comparison entirely.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Backblaze B2 to Mux?
faq.a: Technically you can move between any two tools in the same category, but effort varies: leaving Backblaze B2 is moderate effort at medium lock-in (S3-compatible, portable), and onboarding to Mux involves medium lock-in (Subscription video infra, standard). Plan for data export, re-integration, and a parallel-run window rather than a hard cutover.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Backblaze B2 or Mux?
faq.a: On the 0-5 developer experience score in our data, Mux rates 5/5 while Backblaze B2 rates 4/5, giving Mux the edge. That score is a rough summary; actual day-to-day DX depends on your language, deployment target, and tolerance for edge cases. Prototype both to validate.

faq.q: Is Mux a good alternative to Backblaze B2?
faq.a: Yes, Mux targets the same job Backblaze B2 does — image/video heavy applications — so functionally it slots in as an alternative. Pricing model (subscription from $20/mo vs subscription from $0) and lock-in (medium lock-in (Subscription video infra, standard) vs medium lock-in (S3-compatible, portable)) usually decide which is the better fit. Validate on your actual workload. Note: Mux is explicitly not for small projects.
